mnemonics: The world state is like a massive, shared Google Doc for a blockchain. Everyone has the latest version (the current state), and any change (a transaction) updates it for everyone, creating a new final version.
keyPoints: The world state represents the current data state of all accounts and smart contracts on the blockchain at a given time.
It is a mapping between account addresses and their current state (balance, storage, code).
It is stored as a Merkle Patricia Trie, allowing for efficient and secure verification of state data.
commonMistakes: Confusing the world state with the blockchain itself; the blockchain is the history of transactions, while the world state is the current result of those transactions.
Assuming the entire world state is stored on every node; while every node can verify it, full archival nodes store it, but lightweight clients do not.
